content.full_text:
August 19, 1991

Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Dear Sirs:

The California Association of Hospitals and Health Systems (CAHHS) is seeking written advice regarding the limitations and reporting requirements for gifts under the Political Reform Act. CAHHS will be holding its Annual Meeting in October which is health related in nature.

We are interested in offering a complimentary registration fee to officials or designated employees whose duties are directly related to health issues. The $300 registration fee is for the entire meeting, of which they are invited only to attend sessions that would be informative to them in their job capacity. Items of value, other than registration, such as food, lodging or transportation, would be reported as gifts if paid by CAHHS.

Question: *If CAHHS, a lobbyist employer, offers a complimentary registration fee of $300 to officials or designated employees to attend our organization's conference, will the officials or designated employees be receiving a "gift" or is it considered "informational material." The registration fee does include some meals.*

Due to the date of the meeting, we urgently request a response by September 19, 1991.

A copy of the agenda for officials and designated employees is attached for your reference. Please advise our office of your advice on this issue. If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to contact me.

Sincerely,

Terry Majuk
Legislative Assistant

/tm

Attachment

ANNUAL MEETING AT-A-GLANCE

"Summit at the Dome"

TUESDAY, OCTOBER 29, 1991 (Governance Symposium)
9:30 a.m. - 12:30 p.m. Governance Symposium Welcome & General Session - Hyatt Regency Hotel
2:00 p.m. - 4:30 p.m. Governance Symposium General Session - Hyatt Regency Hotel

WEDNESDAY, OCTOBER 30, 1991
10:00 a.m. - 11:50 p.m. Opening Session - featuring "The Capital Gang" political commentary - Convention Center
1:00 p.m. - 1:50 p.m. Walker Fellowship Reports - Convention Center
2:00 p.m. - 4:30 p.m. General Sessions - Convention Center
• HCFA Administrator Gail Wilensky (invited)
• Congressman Richard Gephardt, (D-Missouri) (invited)
• W. Mitchell - inspirational management speaker addressing overcoming challenges in health care

THURSDAY, OCTOBER 31, 1991
9:00 a.m. - 12:00 noon General Sessions - Convention Center
• Governor Pete Wilson (invited)
• Annual Business Meeting
• Legislative leader addressing health insurance reform
